plan.summary: Implement discernible text for 6 buttons in `index.html` to resolve the 'button-name' accessibility issue, ensuring screen reader users can understand their purpose.

steps.description: Manually inspect the `index.html` file to identify the 6 instances of buttons that are missing discernible text, as flagged by the 'button-name' accessibility rule. Understand the context and intended functionality of each button to determine the most appropriate accessible name.
steps.details: Use browser developer tools or a text editor to locate `<button>` elements or elements with `role='button'` that lack visible text content, an `aria-label`, or an `aria-labelledby` attribute. Pay close attention to icon-only buttons or buttons whose text is visually hidden but needs to be exposed to assistive technologies.

steps.description: For each of the 6 identified buttons in `index.html`, add an appropriate accessible name. This can be achieved by adding visible text content within the `<button>` tags, or by using an `aria-label` attribute for buttons that are purely iconic or have visually hidden text. If a visible label already exists nearby, use `aria-labelledby` to associate it.
steps.details: Example for an icon-only button: Change `<button class="icon-search"></button>` to `<button class="icon-search" aria-label="Search"></button>`. Example for a button with hidden text: Ensure the hidden text is correctly exposed or add `aria-label`. Prioritize visible text content where possible, as it benefits all users.

steps.description: Perform automated accessibility testing on the modified `index.html` using tools like Axe DevTools or Lighthouse to verify that the 'button-name' issue is resolved for all 6 instances. Ensure no new accessibility violations are introduced.
steps.details: Open the `index.html` file in a web browser, navigate to the pages containing the affected buttons, and run an accessibility audit. Confirm that the 'button-name' rule no longer flags any issues.

steps.description: Conduct manual testing using a screen reader (e.g., NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver) to ensure that the implemented button names are correctly announced and provide a clear, concise, and accurate description of the button's purpose to screen reader users.
steps.details: Navigate through the page using keyboard and screen reader commands. Listen to how each of the 6 fixed buttons is announced. Verify that the announced name matches the intended function and is not redundant or confusing.

steps.description: Submit the modified `index.html` file for code review to ensure adherence to accessibility best practices, project coding standards, and to confirm the effectiveness of the implemented fixes.
steps.details: Ensure the changes are well-documented and clearly explain the rationale behind the chosen accessible naming strategy for each button.

risks: Incorrect or ambiguous `aria-label` values could lead to misleading information for screen reader users, making the button's purpose unclear.

Introducing redundant labels if an `aria-label` is added to an element that already has a visible label or is implicitly labeled, potentially creating a confusing or verbose experience for screen reader users.
Overlooking some of the 6 instances during manual inspection, leading to an incomplete resolution of the accessibility bug and requiring further fixes.
Visual regression if visible text is added to buttons that were previously icon-only, requiring CSS adjustments to maintain the intended design.
plan.rollback_strategy: Revert the changes made to `index.html` to the previous version from version control (e.g., Git). This will restore the file to its state before the accessibility fixes were applied.
plan.success_criteria: All 6 instances of the 'button-name' accessibility issue in `index.html` are resolved as confirmed by automated accessibility tools (e.g., Axe DevTools) and manual screen reader testing. Screen reader users can clearly understand the purpose of each button.
plan.original_request:
Fix this accessibility issue:

**Problem**: button-name
**Description**: Ensure buttons have discernible text
**Severity**: medium
**Affected files**: index.html
**Instances**: 6
**Reference**: https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/button-name?application=axeAPI
